author: jih2nn
content:
I think both arguments make sense. After slept on this discussion, I'd lean toward readability over testability.

I do agree that this is about striking a balance. At the same time, I do think some parts of the spec call for an improvement. (I'm not exactly referring to the PRs that I opened.) But it seems our users, i.e., client devs, have strong preferences for not unbundling everything. I believe we can achieved this not necessarily limiting testability too much.

One way I can think of is analyzing input and output space. In the above example, from first principles thinking, what's important is making sure the total input space of A, B and C are covered IMHO. This might require more effort without unbundling everything, but I think this is justified. As a user, your first read is the spec, not tests. I'd like to explore this route before putting the complexity to users.
